# validate.balanced_accuracy { #tspaint.validate.balanced_accuracy }

```python
tspaint.validate.balanced_accuracy(
    tracks,
    truth_states,
    samples=None,
    exclude_missing=True,
    K=2,
)
```

Mean of per-true-class painting accuracies.

Robust to class imbalance: an uninformative painter (argmax tie-broken to one
class) scores ~0.5 rather than the majority-class fraction. The honest "does
it discriminate?" metric.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**tracks**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[Segment](`tspaint.output.Segment`)\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Posterior segment tracks per sample.

<code>[**truth_states**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[int](`int`), [list](`list`)\[[tuple](`tuple`)\[[float](`float`), [float](`float`), [int](`int`)\]\]\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   True ancestry-state tracts per sample (e.g. from :func:`map_truth`).

<code>[**samples**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[iterable](`iterable`)\[[int](`int`)\]]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Samples to score; defaults to all keys of ``tracks``.

<code>[**exclude_missing**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[bool](`bool`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [True]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   If True, skip segments tagged :data:`tspaint.output.MISSING_INFO`.

<code>[**K**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[int](`int`)]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [2]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Number of ancestry states.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[float](`float`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Mean over present classes of the span-weighted per-class accuracy, or ``nan`` if no class is present.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> from tspaint.output import Segment, INFORMATIVE
>>> import numpy as np
>>> tracks = {0: [Segment(0, 10, np.array([0.9, 0.1]), INFORMATIVE),
...               Segment(10, 20, np.array([0.2, 0.8]), INFORMATIVE)]}
>>> truth = {0: [(0, 10, 0), (10, 20, 1)]}
>>> balanced_accuracy(tracks, truth)
1.0
```
